steps.title: Identify the defining requirement
steps.content: The question asks which choice “emphasizes how the two works are similar”—meaning we need language that actively groups or highlights what the two works share in common, not differences or isolated facts.

steps.title: Match the rhetorical approach to the requirement
steps.content:
Choice A explicitly uses “both” to group the works together and identifies acrylic as a shared medium, directly emphasizing their similarity.

The other three fail to emphasize similarity by treating the works separately, contrasting them, or identifying a difference.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
